Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to transform a raw Windows system activity log into a structured process mining event log This involves identifying cases process instances defining activities standardized actions and extracting relevant attributes timestamps case IDs etc The output must be suitable for process mining tools like Celonis UiPath Process Mining etc and include an explanation of the logic used

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Format TimestampActionTypeAttributes Actions FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE Applications Microsoft Word Google Chrome Adobe Acrobat Microsoft Excel Time Span 085950 to 090815 about 9 minutes Context A user working on documents emails and reports

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Case Identification Strategy The Tricky Part Option A One Case Per Session Treat the entire 9minute block as one single case Morning Work Session Pros Simple Cons Process mining tools usually look for repetitive patterns Option B One Case Per DocumentTask Group events based on the specific artifact Decision Option B TaskBased is the most valuable for process mining Task 1 Drafting Document1 docx Task 2 Replying to Email about Annual Meeting Task 3 Reviewing ReportDraft pdf Task 4 Updating Budget2024 xlsx Task 5 Finalizing QuarterlyReport docx

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait looking closer at the sequence Refinement on Cases Case 1 Drafting Document 1 Includes initial typing switching away coming back and closing Wait is Drafting Document 1 one case or two It starts at 0900 Interrupted by Email 090145 Resumed at 090600 Ends at 090700 Process Mining Logic Usually interruptions break a case Critique Splitting Document1 into Intro and Review makes it hard to see the full lifecycle Better Approach Case DocumentFile Case Doc1 Includes all events related to Document1 docx Problem The Document1 events are split by time Solution This is a LongRunning Process or Interrupted Process Decision Distinct Case IDs for Distinct Tasks Case DraftDoc1Part1 Case ReplyEmail Case ReviewPDF Case UpdateBudget Case DraftDoc1Part2 Case FinalizeQuarterly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Best Logic Group by FileApplication Combination Case Doc1Work All events involving Document1 docx Case EmailWork All events involving Email Inbox Case PDFWork All events involving ReportDraft pdf Case BudgetWork All events involving Budget2024 xlsx Case QuarterlyWork All events involving QuarterlyReport docx Handling the gap in Doc1 Keep the events in one case ID CaseDoc1 even if there are other events in between Standard Approach Split by ApplicationArtifact focus Case 1 TaskDoc1Intro 0900 0901 Case 2 TaskEmailReply 090145 090320

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Process Mining Best Practice Usually the SWITCH event represents the transition attach it to the Next Case Review against Constraints Case ID unique Yes Activity Name descriptive Yes Timestamp included Yes Explanation provided Yes Narrative coherent Yes SelfCorrection Merging Document1 into one case makes more sense for Process Mining context where we want to see the lifecycle However standard process mining tools often treat gaps as wait times Therefore Splitting them is the correct approach for accurate process discovery

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Finalizing Activity Names FOCUS FocusWindow TYPING EditContent SAVE SaveWork SWITCH SwitchApplication CLICK PerformAction SCROLL NavigateDocument HIGHLIGHT MarkInformation CLOSE CloseWindow SWITCH event assignment assign SWITCH to the Destination Case the one being switched to because it represents the initiation of the new context standard for Entry events

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: From the log sequences infer general activity mappings like TYPING KeysDraft intro paragraph Activity EditContent CLICK ActionOpen Email Activity OpenEmail CLICK ActionSend Email Activity SendEmail CLICK ActionReply to Email Activity ComposeReply
